phase0_6_stale_prose.note: All STALE/UNRESOLVABLE rows are the linter's own T1-T4 negative-test fixtures in lib/test/run.sh (deliberately-constructed stale-prose corpus, ~line 24680+) plus doc-comment two-item references in stale-prose-lint.py. Out-of-scope test corpus, not defects.

phase3_findings.description: documented_falsehood: _run_git docstring claims errors=replace keeps a non-ASCII byte from detonating the whole pass and that only an unreadable rev is exit-2; under strict-ASCII (C/POSIX) stdout the write path still exits 2 (UnicodeEncodeError). Self-verified by repro.
test_gap: the non-ASCII decode/encode path (the F-1 hardening) had zero coverage; a revert ships GREEN.
_run_git 'only an unreadable rev is exit-2' reads as incomplete (git-unavailable also exit-2). Corroborates the documented_falsehood.
InternalError docstring says 'git failure' but only unreadable-rev raises it (ambiguous/charitable).
R3b never emitted as a rule token (collapses to R3). Not a falsehood; drift-proof doc clause added.
verdict-row is a stringly-typed positional 5-tuple; verdict drives exit code but is enforced nowhere (fail-open IF a future typo is introduced). No live bug.
git show non-zero -> UNRESOLVABLE discards git stderr, conflating benign absence with a real git error (fail-open for one file). Matches prior-run deferred F-6.
strict UTF-8 decode of stdin diff aborts whole pass — but fails closed, and the docstring documents 'non-UTF-8 diff bytes -> exit 2' as intentional.
parse_diff can drop added lines when content begins '++ '. Prior-run deferred F-5; minimal likelihood.
_adjacent_list_count below-branch and deleted-at-rev UNRESOLVABLE '-' path untested (minor coverage).

Cloud-runner leading-token shape for the helper invocation — verify via matcher-probe.yml, fail-safe (arm a) otherwise. Cannot verify locally (matcher semantics only provable in the real cloud action).

fix_decisions.evidence: over-grade shape 1 (fail-closed / no live bug): the code is correct today; the fail-open is hypothetical (a FUTURE typo at an emit site). No wrong output, no corrupted state on any current input. type-design-analyzer itself graded it 'no live bugs, none block'. A NamedTuple refactor is optional polish on a python3-stdlib-minimal helper; recorded evaluation, not auto-demoted.

fix_decisions.claim_text: git show non-zero discards stderr / conflates absence with git error
fix_decisions.evidence: prior-run deferred F-6 (iter-1.json committed in the PR); blast radius low (--rev pre-validated up front, so common non-zero is legitimate absence); UNRESOLVABLE never gates.

fix_decisions.claim_text: strict stdin-diff decode aborts whole pass
fix_decisions.evidence: intentional and documented (module header line 53 lists 'non-UTF-8 diff bytes' as an exit-2 cause); fails CLOSED (exit 2 + stderr + visible arm-c note), a loud bounded stop, not a silent miss. Distinct from the write path (this fix) which was undocumented-and-fail-open-shaped.
prior-run deferred F-5; prose beginning '++ ' is near-nonexistent; degrades toward no-finding, not a false gate.
minor coverage gaps (criticality 3); the below-branch fallback and the '-' sentinel are simple and low-risk; not worth blocking convergence. Recorded for a future coverage pass.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Phase 0.6 helper leading-token invocation shape permitted in cloud review runner
fix_decisions.evidence: cloud matcher semantics are only provable via matcher-probe.yml in the real cloud action (repo convention; local probes invalid). Mirrors the established config-get.sh anchor shape; grant added to both TOOLS lines; degradation arm (a) fails safe naming the remedy. Parked for a matcher-probe run, not a code defect.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Phase 0.6 flags the linter's own T1-T4 negative-test fixtures as STALE
fix_decisions.evidence: the STALE rows (~line 24680+) are the linter's deliberately-constructed stale-prose test corpus; they MUST contain stale prose to test detection. Fixing them would break the tests. Inherent property of a deterministic line scanner reviewing its own test file; one-time on this PR only.

verdict: REJECT (self-contradicting-diff documented_falsehood) -> fixed in this iteration
